name: large-report-editor
description: Edits and compiles very large Markdown research reports using surgical patches, structure JSON, and legal‑grade citation rules.
capabilities: Applies surgical patches to very large Markdown files without losing context
Uses structure JSON to understand document hierarchy and relationships
Implements legal-grade citation rules with StableID and page anchors
Creates automatic backups before major edits in Archive directory
Processes chunked edits with full context preservation
author: ACSKamloops
securitySummary: Static analysis detected patterns related to URL references, file paths, and markdown formatting that are false positives. The skill is a document editing tool for Markdown research reports with no actual network calls, file system exploitation, or code execution risks. All detected patterns stem from metadata fields and documentation references.
valueStatement: Editing very large Markdown research reports is challenging with context loss and citation errors. This skill uses surgical patches, structure JSON, and legal-grade citation rules to update documents safely while preserving all references and page anchors.
userTitle: Edit Large Research Reports with Precision
limitations: Requires documents to follow standard Markdown formatting conventions
Cannot process encrypted or password-protected documents
Best suited for research reports with formal citation structures
Large files may require multiple edit passes for complex changes

useCases.title: Update Research Paper Citations
useCases.description: Add, remove, or update citations in a 200-page academic paper while preserving all cross-references and maintaining proper citation numbering throughout the document.

useCases.title: Revise Technical Documentation
useCases.description: Make targeted edits to large technical specification documents or API documentation without breaking internal links, code examples, or reference sections.

useCases.title: Compile Annual Reports
useCases.description: Merge updates from multiple contributors into a comprehensive annual report while maintaining consistent formatting, table of contents, and cross-references.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Report Edit
promptTemplates.prompt: Edit the large report at {file_path} to update section {section_name}. Make the following changes: {list_of_changes}. Ensure all citations and cross-references remain valid.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to make specific edits to a named section

promptTemplates.title: Citation Update
promptTemplates.prompt: Update all citations in {file_path} to use the new format: Author (Year). Replace existing citations while preserving StableIDs and page anchors. Report any broken references.
promptTemplates.scenario: When standardizing citation format across a document

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Section Revision
promptTemplates.prompt: Review {file_path} and make surgical edits to sections {section_list}. For each change, apply the surgical patch method, cite new statements with StableID + page anchor, and backup to Archive before saving.
promptTemplates.scenario: When making coordinated edits across multiple sections

promptTemplates.title: Context-Aware Refactoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor the discussion section in {file_path} to improve clarity and flow. Use structure JSON to understand current relationships. Preserve all citations and maintain logical argument structure.
promptTemplates.scenario: When improving document structure while preserving content

bestPractices: Always work with backups enabled and verify archive creation before major edits
Use structure JSON to understand document hierarchy before making changes
Cite all new content with StableID and page anchors for traceability
antiPatterns: Making wholesale replacements of large sections without surgical patches
Skipping citation rules when adding new content

faq.question: What file sizes can this skill handle?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for documents over 100 pages. Very large files (500+ pages) may require chunked processing with context management.

faq.question: Does this skill work with PDF files?
faq.answer: No, this skill operates on Markdown files only. For PDF editing, convert documents to Markdown format first.

faq.question: How does citation preservation work?
faq.answer: The skill tracks StableIDs and page anchors for each citation. When editing, it verifies citation integrity and reports any broken references.

faq.question: Can I undo changes?
faq.answer: Yes, all edits create backups in 99_Working_Files/Archive/ before saving. You can restore from these backups if needed.

faq.question: What citation formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports standard academic citation formats including APA, MLA, Chicago, and custom StableID + anchor notation.

faq.question: Does this skill work with collaborative documents?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can process documents with tracked changes. It applies edits as new content while preserving existing change markers.
